critique.summary: OpenTTD will no longer be available as a standalone game on Steam but can now be purchased as part of a bundle with Transport Tycoon Deluxe. The game remains free, but new players must buy the bundle to access OpenTTD on Steam.
mainPoints: OpenTTD is now part of a bundle with Transport Tycoon Deluxe on Steam.
Existing players will not be affected and can continue to access updates.
The game remains free, but new players must purchase the bundle to download it.
critique.truthfulness: The article accurately describes the changes to OpenTTD's availability on Steam and clarifies that the game remains free, albeit requiring a purchase for new players. It provides clear information about the bundle and reassures existing players of continued access.
considerations: The article does not mention the potential impact on the player base due to the new purchasing requirement.
It does not address how this change might affect the game's community or modding support.

comments_critique.summary: The discussion revolves around the implications of Atari's changes to the distribution of OpenTTD on Steam, with varying opinions on the fairness and impact of these changes. Some users express concern over Atari's role, while others see potential benefits in the arrangement.

comments.author: ndiddy
comments.text: Obviously having OpenTTD available for free on Steam would jeopardize Atari's paid rerelease of Transport Tycoon Deluxe, so I think this is a good comp...
comments.analysis: This comment provides a balanced perspective on the situation, acknowledging the potential conflict between free and paid versions.

comments.author: TuxMark5
comments.text: This doesn't feel right for me. OpenTTD is so much superior in every way compared to the original TTD, that noone in their right mind would ever p...
comments.analysis: While passionate, this comment may overstate the superiority of OpenTTD and lacks consideration of the broader context.

comments.author: nout

comments.author: binaryturtle
comments.text: So does OpenTTD gets a part of the generated income then? The team has costs too to support the game.
comments.analysis: This comment raises an important financial question but lacks depth in exploring the implications.

comments.author: sarru_kin
comments.analysis: This comment offers a simplistic solution without addressing the complexities of the situation.

comments.text: Surprisingly, this is an interesting outcome. Atari could have been worse (better too).

comments.author: xyzzy_plugh
comments.text: It would be nice if someone could provide some explanation as to why this situation is necessary. Did Atari's lawyers go full tilt?
